notes: ACS PUMS TYPE=1 (housing unit resident) DIS=1 AGEP 18-64. Denominator = all PWD 18-64 including GQ.

notes: ACS PUMS TYPE=3 (non-institutional GQ: college dorms + military barracks + shelters) DIS=1 AGEP 18-64.

Living in Nursing Home Age 18 to 64: Relative
notes: ACS PUMS DIS=1 AGEP 18-64 institutional GQ subtype = nursing home/skilled nursing. Verify exact GQ type code in 2024 PUMS codebook. 65+ removed per 2024 revision.

notes: ACS PUMS DIS=1 AGEP 18-64 institutional GQ subtype = correctional facility. Verify exact GQ type code.

indicator_label: HCBS vs. Institutional Spending Ratio: Relative
notes: Source: KFF Custom State Report (HCBS 2024.csv). FFS data from CMS-64. Formula: Home Health & Personal Care spend / (Home Health & PC + Nursing Facilities + ICF-ID). FFS only — excludes managed-care HCBS (no CMS-64 breakout within managed care). Data year: FY2024. Community Resource OLD version.

Source: KFF Custom State Report (HCBS 2024.csv). Formula: Home Health & Personal Care FFS spend (FY2024) / People with Disabilities Medicaid enrollees (2023). Denominator is all disabled Medicaid enrollees (not just HCBS waiver participants — no unduplicated HCBS count available in file). FFS spending only.

indicator_label: Housing Choice Voucher (Head of Household): Relative
notes: Source: HUD Picture of Subsidized Households (POSH) — huduser.gov/portal/datasets/assthsg.html. State-level total HCV (Section 8) households. OLD version: no disability filter on HoH. Denominator: total disabled population by state (from ACS disability count — e.g. B18101 total with disability). Rate = HCV_HHs / disabled_pop. Most recent: 2023.

Source: HUD POSH (huduser.gov/portal/datasets/assthsg.html). State-level total Public Housing households. OLD version: no disability filter. Denominator: total disabled population by state (same as HCV). Rate = PH_HHs / disabled_pop. Most recent: 2023.
ACS PUMS: household has computer (LAPTOP=1 or DESKTOP=1) any member DIS=1. Confirm PUMS variable names in 2024 codebook.
ACS PUMS: household has smartphone (SMARTPHONE=1) any member DIS=1. Confirm exact variable name in 2024 PUMS codebook.
ACS PUMS: household has internet access (ACCESSINET=1) any member DIS=1. Or S1810_C02 internet row. Confirm in 2024 PUMS codebook.

Source: ACS B18135 row B18135_016E (with disability age 19-64 + private health insurance). Denominator: B18135 'with disability age 19-64 total' row (verify suffix near _016E in 2024 table shell). Variable already present in national_data from prior pull. Age 19-64 per confirmed B18135 row structure.
PWOD: corresponding B18135 no-disability age 19-64 private health insurance row.

Source: ACS B18135_017E (with disability age 19-64 + public health insurance — includes Medicare + Medicaid + CHIP + other public). Variable confirmed present in national_data from main pipeline ACS run. Denominator: B18135 'with disability 19-64 total' row. Consolidated indicator replacing separate Medicare/Medicaid indicators. Scoring direction: higher_better (public coverage access is positive for disabled people).
PWOD variable pct_cni_19to64_nodis_publichealth confirmed present in national_data from main pipeline ACS run.

Source: ACS B18135_018E (with disability age 19-64 + no health insurance). Denominator: B18135 'with disability 19-64 total' row. Variable already present in national_data from prior pull. Age 19-64 per confirmed B18135 row structure.
PWOD: B18135 no-disability age 19-64 no-insurance row.

Source: FBI NIBRS 2024 — Table 28 (Crimes Against Property). Downloaded to external_data/. Variable: property_crime_rate = (Burglary + Larceny/Theft + Motor Vehicle Theft) / Population Covered × 100,000. Arson excluded. Rate denominator = population covered by NIBRS-reporting agencies (NOT total state pop). 2024 low-participation states: FL (~62% covered), PA (~51% covered), AK (~61% covered) — flagged in fbi_crime_2024.csv via fbi_coverage_note column. Data added to national_data via 01b Section B + INTEGRATION (April 2026).

Source: FBI NIBRS 2024 — Table 27 (Crimes Against Persons) + Table 28 (robbery). Variable: violent_crime_rate = (Assault + Homicide + Sex Offenses [T27] + Robbery [T28]) / Population Covered × 100,000. Note: NIBRS Assault Offenses includes all assault levels (not just aggravated); Sex Offenses is broader than legacy UCR rape. Same 2024 low-participation caveats as property crime (FL, PA, AK). Data added to national_data via 01b Section B + INTEGRATION (April 2026).

notes: ACS B18131. Employed civilians 16+ with disability. Denominator = all civilians 16+ with disability (employed + unemployed + NILF).

Base: ACS PUMS PERNP DIS=1 AGEP 18-64 ESR=1or2. Use median. COL adjustment: BEA Regional Price Parities (RPPs) — bea.gov. Most recent RPP: 2022. Formula: COL_adj = median_PERNP / (state_RPP / 100). Confirm full-time year-round filter with team.
PWOD: same PUMS pull without DIS filter + BEA RPP.
Base: ACS PUMS HINCP any member DIS=1. Use median. COL: BEA RPP same as individual income. Disability HH = any member DIS=1.
PWOD: PUMS HINCP no member DIS=1 + BEA RPP.

Source: ACS B18130 age-split count columns already in national_data from main pipeline. pct_cni_dis_poverty is a DERIVED all-ages aggregate computed in script 02 as: (pop_cni_under18_poverty_dis + pop_cni_18to64_poverty_dis + pop_cni_65plus_poverty_dis) / (pop_cni_under18_dis + pop_cni_18to64_dis + pop_cni_65plus_dis). Replaces age-split indicators per 2024 revision.
PWOD: pct_cni_nodis_poverty derived the same way from nodis count columns in national_data. Computed in script 02 alongside PWD version.

Source: ACS B18131. Variable confirmed present in national_data from main pipeline ACS run. Denominator = labor force participants (employed_dis + unemployed_dis). Unemployment rate = unemployed_dis / labor_force_dis. New 2024 indicator.

PWOD variable is pct_cni_16plus_nodis_unemployed (note: nodis precedes unemployed in national_data naming convention — differs from PWD column order). Confirmed present in national_data from main pipeline ACS run.
ACS B18131. Not in labor force rows for disability population 16+. Denominator = all civilians 16+ with disability.

indicator_label: SSI as Percent of 1BR Fair Market Rent: Relative
notes: Source 1 — SSI: SSA ASR 2024 Table 39 average monthly payment (December 2024) for under-65 recipients by state — combined federal + state supplement average (ssi_asr24.xlsx). Using average actual payment rather than federal maximum because it captures real state supplement differences. Source 2 — FMR: HUD FY2025 FMRs 1BR (FY25_FMRs_revised.xlsx/csv) — population-weighted mean of county 1BR FMRs by state. Formula: (avg_monthly_SSI / pop_wtd_state_1BR_FMR) x 100. Higher ratio = SSI covers more of FMR cost; lower_better scoring per convention.

Source: ACS PUMS GRPIP >= 30 (gross rent 30%+ of income) DIS=1 TEN=3 (renter). Denominator = all renter HHs any member DIS=1. CONFIRMED: disability-specific PUMS pull. Alternative if not feasible: B25070_007E+ / B25070_001E (general population).
PWOD: PUMS GRPIP >= 30 DIS=0 TEN=3.

Source: ACS PUMS OCPIP >= 30 (monthly owner costs 30%+ of income) DIS=1 TEN=1or2 (owner). Denominator = all owner HHs any member DIS=1. CONFIRMED: disability-specific PUMS pull. Alternative if not feasible: B25091 (general population).
PWOD: PUMS OCPIP >= 30 DIS=0 TEN=1or2.
